incident_sources.label: BleepingComputer — M&S NTDS.dit / ESXi reporting (primary)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: specialist press primary — single chain of unnamed sources
incident_sources.text: Sources familiar with the matter told BleepingComputer that the attackers obtained a copy of the NTDS.dit Active Directory database and subsequently encrypted the company's VMware ESXi virtual machines. The reporting attributes the intrusion to Scattered Spider.

incident_sources.label: Reuters — M&S cyberattack round-up (cites BleepingComputer)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: major outlet round-up — repeats specialist-press detail, adds no independent sourcing
incident_sources.text: According to a report by BleepingComputer, the attackers behind the Marks & Spencer incident stole the NTDS.dit Active Directory database and encrypted VMware ESXi virtual machines. Reuters has not independently verified these technical details.

incident_sources.label: The Guardian — M&S hackers summary (cites specialist press)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: general news summary — derivative of the same origin
incident_sources.text: Specialist cyber-security press has reported that the Marks & Spencer attackers obtained the NTDS.dit credential database before encrypting ESXi virtual machines. The Guardian's summary is based on that coverage.

incident_sources.label: Sky News — M&S cyberattack explainer (cites specialist press)
incident_sources.incentive_bias: broadcast explainer — derivative of the same origin
incident_sources.text: Reports in the technical press say the hackers who hit Marks & Spencer took a copy of the NTDS.dit database and went on to encrypt the firm's ESXi virtual machines. This explainer repeats those reported details.
